name: BTC Trend Reset Continuation v1
edge_mechanism: deeper intraday pullback reset that reclaims fast value before continuation
thesis: BTC 1h is the cleaner similar-asset lane for testing whether a deeper reset continuation can still work without shallow EMA-touch fragility. The edge should come from repaired pullbacks that re-establish directional control after inventory reset.
invalidation_condition: Abort if reset-and-reclaim setups are too sparse, if reclaimed pullbacks do not extend, or if the intraday lane behaves as pure chop with no second leg.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price pulls back materially within an existing directional move, reclaims fast value, and resumes with a modest second leg.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Even in mixed intraday structure, BTC often repairs deeper pullbacks more cleanly than alts. That makes it a good continuation control lane.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: BTC 1h offers enough structure for reset logic without the thinness of smaller high-beta names.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If the market is too range-bound, repaired pullbacks will look valid but fail to produce continuation travel.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If continuation exists but is short-lived, compress exits or add faster de-risking before touching pullback logic.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Selective repaired-pullback entries with decent first-target hit rate and occasional runner contribution.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: BTC 1h and at least one cross-lane show real post-cost continuation after reclaim with acceptable density.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, too-sparse output, or reclaimed pullbacks that repeatedly stall immediately.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; pullback, reclaim, and participation check align
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by using moderate reset depth rather than rare washout definitions

test_lanes.why_included: Cleanest continuation-control lane in the current basket.
test_lanes.continue_if: Reset-reclaim trades show credible density and positive post-cost continuation.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: The lane stays too choppy and reclaimed pullbacks never extend.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: A decent number of repaired pullbacks followed by modest second legs.

test_lanes.why_included: Checks whether the continuation mechanism transfers to the basket primary lane.
test_lanes.continue_if: ETH shows comparable repaired pullback behavior.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: The mechanism collapses on ETH and only works on BTC.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Slightly noisier but still tradable repaired continuations.

test_lanes.why_included: Tests whether the same grammar survives a higher-beta lane.
test_lanes.continue_if: AXS preserves the reset-continuation shape without becoming chaotic.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Fewer entries but larger post-reclaim legs than majors.

long: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.12 * ATR_14 by selling 15%
short: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.12 * ATR_14 by covering 15%
